questions.passage: The placebo effect has long puzzled medical researchers. When patients are given an inert substance and told it is an effective treatment, a significant proportion of them report genuine improvements in their symptoms — improvements that can sometimes be measured objectively. For many years, scientists assumed the effect would vanish once patients learned they were receiving a placebo. Recent studies, however, have challenged this assumption. In a series of clinical trials, patients suffering from chronic lower back pain were openly told that they were taking sugar pills with no active ingredients, yet they still reported meaningful pain relief compared to a control group receiving no treatment at all. This "open-label" placebo effect suggests that the mere act of engaging in a treatment ritual, combined with a doctor's attention and care, can mobilize the body's own healing mechanisms regardless of the patient's conscious belief in the substance. The mind, it seems, does not need to be deceived to generate a therapeutic response.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

The placebo effect can produce (A) health improvements even when patients are fully (B) of receiving an inactive treatment, suggesting the body's healing responses can be activated through the treatment ritual itself.

explanations.correct: (A) genuine — 지문의 "genuine improvements in their symptoms — improvements that can sometimes be measured objectively"가 효과가 '실제적임'을 직접 뒷받침한다. (B) aware — "patients were openly told that they were taking sugar pills"와 "the mind does not need to be deceived"가 환자가 플라시보임을 '알면서도' 효과가 나타남을 보여준다.

(B) suspicious가 오답 — 지문은 환자가 의심하는 것이 아니라 사실을 '공개적으로 고지받은(openly told)' 상태임을 강조한다.
(A) delayed가 오답 — 지문에는 효과 발현 지연에 대한 언급이 없다. (B) ignorant도 오답 — 환자는 플라시보임을 알고 있으므로 'ignorant(모르는)'는 틀렸다.
(A) imaginary가 오답 — 지문은 개선 효과가 'objectively measured(객관적으로 측정 가능)'하다고 명시한다. (B) convinced도 오답 — open-label 연구의 핵심은 약효를 확신하지 않아도 효과가 나타난다는 것이다.
explanations.key_point: (A)의 핵심 근거: 지문의 'genuine improvements ... objectively measured'. (B)의 핵심 근거: open-label 설계—환자가 플라시보임을 알아도(aware) 효과가 발생한다는 역설이 지문 전체의 주제다.

questions.passage: Most people rate their own driving ability as above average — a statistical impossibility given that half of all drivers must, by definition, be below average. This tendency to overestimate one's own competence relative to others is known as the Dunning-Kruger effect, and it manifests across a remarkably wide range of skills and domains. What makes this cognitive bias particularly problematic is that the very skills needed to accurately evaluate one's performance are the same skills that are lacking in poor performers. A person who knows little about grammar, for instance, also lacks the grammatical knowledge required to recognize the errors in their own writing. Conversely, highly skilled individuals often underestimate their abilities, assuming that tasks they find easy must also be easy for others. The result is a paradox: those who know the least tend to be the most confident, while those who know the most are frequently the most uncertain about the extent of their knowledge.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

The Dunning-Kruger effect describes how low-skilled individuals tend to (A) their abilities while experts tend to (B) theirs, because accurate self-evaluation depends on the very competence one lacks.

explanations.correct: (A) overrate — 지문의 "tendency to overestimate one's own competence"와 운전 실력을 '평균 이상'이라 자평하는 통계적 불가능 예시가 미숙련자가 자신을 '과대평가'함을 뒷받침한다. (B) doubt — "highly skilled individuals often underestimate their abilities"와 "those who know the most are frequently the most uncertain"이 전문가가 자신의 능력을 '의심'한다는 근거다.
wrong: (A) underrate가 오답 — 지문은 미숙련자가 자신을 과소평가하는 것이 아니라 'overestimate(과대평가)'한다고 명시한다.
(B) celebrate가 오답 — 지문은 전문가가 자신의 능력을 'underestimate(과소평가)'하고 'uncertain(불확실해한다)'고 하므로 '축하한다'는 의미와 거리가 멀다.
(A) acknowledge가 오답 — 지문의 핵심은 미숙련자가 자신의 한계를 인정하지 '못한다'는 것이다.
(A) conceal이 오답 — 던닝-크루거 효과는 의도적 은폐가 아니라 인지 편향, 즉 스스로 인식하지 못하는 과대평가다.
explanations.key_point: (A): 미숙련자는 'overrate' — 지문 직접 인용: "overestimate one's own competence". (B): 전문가는 'doubt' — 지문 직접 인용: "the most uncertain about the extent of their knowledge".

questions.passage: Laughter is one of the most universal forms of human expression, appearing across all known cultures. While it is commonly associated with humor, research suggests that humor accounts for only a small fraction of laughter in everyday social interactions. Most laughter, studies show, occurs not in response to jokes but during ordinary conversation, frequently following unexceptional remarks such as "I have to go now" or "I see what you mean." This pattern has led psychologists to propose that laughter functions primarily as a social signal rather than a purely humorous response. By laughing, people signal their approval, warmth, and willingness to engage with the person they are talking to. Laughter synchronizes emotional states between individuals, creating a sense of shared experience and mutual understanding. Groups that laugh together tend to report higher levels of trust and cohesion. In this light, laughter can be understood as a form of vocal grooming — a way of reinforcing social bonds much as primates reinforce bonds through physical grooming.

questions.passage: Psychologists have long noted that the more familiar we become with a stimulus — whether a word, a face, a melody, or an abstract shape — the more positively we tend to evaluate it, even in the absence of any conscious recognition that we have encountered it before. This tendency, known as the mere exposure effect, has been demonstrated in hundreds of experimental studies spanning multiple cultures and sensory modalities. In one classic experiment, participants were briefly shown a series of Chinese characters at speeds too fast for conscious recognition. When later asked to rate their liking of the same characters alongside new ones, they consistently rated the previously seen characters more favorably, despite reporting no memory of having seen them. The effect appears to operate below the level of conscious awareness, suggesting that familiarity itself — independent of any rational evaluation — is sufficient to generate a preference. Advertisers and politicians have long exploited this finding: simply repeating an image or a name, even without accompanying positive content, can increase the public's favorable response.

questions.passage: When people witness an emergency in the presence of a large crowd, they are statistically less likely to intervene than if they are the sole observer. In a now-famous series of experiments, psychologists John Darley and Bibb Latané staged an apparent epileptic seizure and varied the number of bystanders present. They found that 85% of participants who believed they were the only witness attempted to help, compared to just 31% when five other bystanders were present. This phenomenon, known as the bystander effect, operates through two related mechanisms. First, individuals in a crowd experience diffusion of responsibility: they assume that someone else will take action, so they personally feel less obligated to do so. Second, when people see that others around them are not reacting, they interpret the situation as less serious than it might actually be, a process called pluralistic ignorance. Together, these mechanisms can transform a crowd from a source of help into an obstacle to it, paradoxically making victims less safe when more people are around.

The bystander effect reveals that a larger crowd can actually (A) the likelihood of someone receiving help, as individuals (B) responsibility and misread others' inaction as a sign that intervention is unnecessary.

questions.passage: Many people pride themselves on their ability to multitask, juggling phone calls, emails, and in-person conversations simultaneously. However, decades of cognitive research tell a different story. The human brain, studies consistently show, is not designed to process multiple demanding tasks at the same time. What we call multitasking is, in reality, a process of rapid task-switching: the brain toggles between activities, and each switch carries a cognitive cost. This cost — often called the "switch cost" — manifests as slower reaction times, higher error rates, and reduced depth of processing. In one study, participants who multitasked during a learning session retained significantly less information than those who focused on a single task. The performance gap widened under more cognitively demanding conditions. Ironically, self-described frequent multitaskers performed worst of all on laboratory tests of multitasking ability, suggesting that habitual task-switching may itself impair the very cognitive skills it is assumed to develop.

questions.passage: Nostalgia — the bittersweet longing for the past — was once classified as a psychological disorder. Swiss physician Johannes Hofer coined the term in 1688 to describe the debilitating homesickness suffered by Swiss mercenaries stationed far from their mountain villages. For the next three centuries, nostalgia was largely viewed as a symptom of weakness or mental instability. Modern psychology, however, has dramatically revised this assessment. A growing body of research indicates that nostalgic reflection serves important psychological functions. When people engage in nostalgic thinking, they tend to experience increased feelings of social connectedness, perceiving their relationships as more meaningful and their lives as more purposeful. Studies have also found that nostalgia acts as a buffer against loneliness: participants induced to feel nostalgic reported feeling less alone and more secure in their sense of self. Rather than pulling people away from the present, nostalgia appears to provide the emotional resources needed to face current challenges with greater confidence and optimism.

questions.passage: Body language plays a decisive but often underappreciated role in negotiation. Research in organizational psychology suggests that negotiators who adopt expansive, open postures — leaning forward, maintaining eye contact, and using deliberate hand gestures — are perceived as more confident and authoritative by their counterparts. These perceptions, in turn, influence the concessions that parties are willing to make. In a series of simulated salary negotiations, participants who were instructed to maintain open body posture secured outcomes that were, on average, 16% more favorable than those who adopted closed, self-protective postures. Intriguingly, the effect operates not only on others but on the negotiator themselves: adopting a dominant posture triggers hormonal changes — specifically, increases in testosterone and decreases in cortisol — that increase the individual's own willingness to take risks and assert their position. The body, it appears, does not merely reflect confidence but actively generates it, creating a feedback loop between physical stance and psychological state.

questions.passage: One of the most persistent challenges in scientific research is the tendency of investigators to unconsciously favor evidence that supports their existing hypotheses while discounting or overlooking data that contradicts them. This cognitive bias, known as confirmation bias, operates at every stage of the research process. In the design phase, researchers may choose measurement instruments or experimental conditions that are more sensitive to expected outcomes. During data collection, anomalous results may be attributed to equipment failure or human error and discarded. In the interpretation phase, patterns consistent with the hypothesis receive disproportionate attention. Peer review, which is supposed to serve as a corrective mechanism, is not immune: reviewers who share a researcher's theoretical commitments are more likely to approve a paper for publication. The consequent distortion of the scientific literature — often called publication bias — means that the published record systematically overstates the strength and consistency of evidence for prevailing theories, leaving scientists with an inflated confidence in claims that may, upon broader examination, be far weaker than they appear.

questions.passage: Planned obsolescence refers to the deliberate design of products with a limited lifespan, ensuring that consumers must replace them sooner than they otherwise would. The strategy, widely adopted by manufacturers from the 1950s onward, takes two principal forms. Functional obsolescence occurs when a product is engineered to break down or malfunction after a predetermined period. Psychological obsolescence, by contrast, does not depend on physical failure; instead, manufacturers release cosmetically updated versions of functional products — a slightly different shape, a new color range, or a redesigned interface — to make existing models feel outdated and undesirable. Critics argue that both forms of planned obsolescence generate enormous volumes of waste, contributing significantly to the environmental burden of consumer culture. Proponents counter that the strategy stimulates innovation and economic growth by continuously creating demand. What is undeniable is that planned obsolescence has fundamentally altered the relationship between consumers and the objects they own, transforming possession from a long-term investment into a temporary arrangement.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

Planned obsolescence, whether achieved through engineering products to (A) or through cosmetic updates that induce dissatisfaction, has reshaped consumer culture while raising serious (B) concerns.

questions.passage: Social media platforms were initially celebrated as tools of democratization, offering citizens direct access to information and enabling grassroots political organization. Over the past decade, however, a growing body of research has documented a troubling side effect: algorithmic curation of content appears to intensify political polarization. Because platforms are designed to maximize user engagement, and because content that provokes strong emotional reactions — particularly outrage and indignation — generates more clicks and shares, recommendation algorithms systematically expose users to material that reinforces and inflames their existing political beliefs. The result is the formation of what scholars call "filter bubbles" or "echo chambers," in which users are insulated from perspectives that challenge their own. Experimental studies have confirmed that extended use of algorithmically curated social media is associated with more extreme political positions and reduced willingness to consider opposing viewpoints. The architecture of connection, it seems, has become an architecture of division.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

Social media algorithms, by prioritizing emotionally (A) content, tend to trap users in echo chambers where their existing views are continuously (B), thereby deepening political divisions.

(A) balanced가 오답 — 에코 챔버는 본질적으로 편향된 정보 환경이므로 '균형 잡힌' 콘텐츠와 반대된다.

questions.passage: Bilingual speakers do not simply maintain two separate language systems that they alternate between; rather, both languages are perpetually active in the mind, competing for selection with every utterance. The practice of switching between languages mid-conversation, known as code-switching, was long dismissed by educators as a sign of linguistic deficiency — evidence that the speaker lacked full mastery of either language. Linguists now understand code-switching very differently. Far from being a failure of competence, code-switching is a highly sophisticated communicative strategy that draws simultaneously on the resources of both languages to achieve effects that would be difficult or impossible in either language alone. Bilinguals switch codes to signal group identity, to express nuances that one language captures better than the other, to soften or intensify an utterance, or simply to fill lexical gaps. Studies have demonstrated that proficient bilinguals code-switch more, not less, than those who are less fluent, suggesting that the practice requires and demonstrates a high level of linguistic control rather than a lack thereof.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

Code-switching, once dismissed as a mark of linguistic (A), is now recognized as a (B) skill that allows bilinguals to exploit the full communicative potential of both languages.

explanations.correct: (A) incompetence — 지문의 과거 인식인 "sign of linguistic deficiency — evidence that the speaker lacked full mastery of either language"가 코드 전환이 언어적 '무능'의 표시로 여겨졌음을 직접 뒷받침한다. (B) sophisticated — 지문의 현재 이해인 "highly sophisticated communicative strategy"가 코드 전환이 '정교한' 기술임을 직접 명시한다.

(B) problematic이 오답 — 지문의 현재 관점은 코드 전환이 문제적이 아니라 'highly sophisticated(매우 정교한)' 전략임을 강조한다.
(A) flexibility가 오답 — 지문에서 코드 전환이 과거에 무시당한 이유는 유연성 때문이 아니라 능력 부족으로 보였기 때문이다. (B) limited도 오답 — 지문은 코드 전환이 두 언어의 '모든 의사소통 잠재력'을 활용한다고 서술한다.

questions.passage: When astronomers in the 1970s mapped the rotation speeds of stars at different distances from the centers of galaxies, they discovered something that Newtonian mechanics could not explain. According to gravitational theory, stars at the outer edges of a galaxy should orbit more slowly than those near the center, just as the outer planets in our solar system orbit the Sun more slowly than the inner ones. Instead, measurements showed that stars throughout a galaxy rotate at roughly the same speed regardless of their distance from the center — and in many cases, the outer stars were moving faster than theory predicted. The only consistent explanation for this behavior was the presence of a vast, invisible mass distributed throughout and around galaxies. Scientists named this hypothetical substance dark matter. Despite decades of effort, no one has directly detected a dark matter particle. Yet the gravitational anomalies that first suggested its existence have been observed in galaxy after galaxy, making dark matter one of the most compelling and persistent mysteries in modern astrophysics.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

The (A) rotation speeds of stars at the outer edges of galaxies, which contradict standard gravitational predictions, have led scientists to (B) an invisible mass known as dark matter, even though it has never been directly detected.

explanations.correct: (A) unexpected — 지문의 "something that Newtonian mechanics could not explain"과 "outer stars were moving faster than theory predicted"가 관측 속도가 '예상치 못한' 것이었음을 뒷받침한다. (B) propose — 지문의 "The only consistent explanation for this behavior was the presence of a vast, invisible mass"와 "Scientists named this hypothetical substance dark matter"가 과학자들이 암흑 물질을 '제안'했음을 보여준다.
wrong: (A) expected가 오답 — 지문은 외부 별들의 속도가 이론적 예측과 달랐음을 명시하므로 '예상된' 것과 정반대다.
(A) decreasing이 오답 — 이론적으로는 속도가 감소해야 했지만(decreasing이 예상), 실제로는 그렇지 않았다. 이는 관측된 결과가 아니라 예측된 결과다. (B) eliminate도 오답 — 과학자들은 암흑 물질 가설을 제거한 것이 아니라 제안했다.
(B) doubt가 오답 — 지문은 과학자들이 암흑 물질의 존재를 의심한 것이 아니라 이를 설명 가설로 '제안'했음을 서술한다.

explanations.key_point: (A): 별들의 속도 = 'Newtonian mechanics could not explain' → unexpected. (B): 과학자들의 반응 = 'hypothetical substance dark matter' → propose.

questions.passage: The ocean has long served as a sink for human waste, but the recent accumulation of plastic debris presents a challenge unlike any before it. Unlike organic matter, most plastics do not biodegrade; instead, they break down into progressively smaller fragments through exposure to ultraviolet radiation and mechanical abrasion. These microplastics — defined as particles smaller than five millimeters — are now found in virtually every marine environment, from surface waters to deep-sea sediments and polar ice. Microplastics are ingested by a wide range of marine organisms, from zooplankton to large fish and marine mammals. Because plastics adsorb persistent organic pollutants from surrounding seawater, organisms that consume them may be exposed to concentrated doses of toxic chemicals. The particles travel up the food chain, accumulating in progressively higher concentrations — a process known as biomagnification. Studies detecting microplastics in commercially sold seafood, drinking water, and even human blood have raised urgent questions about the long-term health implications for human populations.
questions.question:

Microplastics, which (A) in marine environments and travel up the food chain through biomagnification, pose a growing (B) to both marine ecosystems and human health.

questions.passage: Flow is a state of intense psychological absorption in a challenging activity, first described by psychologist Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi. During flow, individuals report a complete loss of self-consciousness, a distortion of time perception, and an intrinsic sense of enjoyment that is independent of external rewards. The state arises at the intersection of high challenge and high skill: tasks that are too easy produce boredom, while tasks that are too difficult produce anxiety. Flow occurs in the narrow band where the demands of a task and the capabilities of the performer are evenly matched. Research has consistently linked flow experiences to higher levels of creative output, improved learning outcomes, and greater overall well-being. Importantly, flow is self-reinforcing: the positive affect associated with the state motivates individuals to engage again in the activities that trigger it, gradually building their skills and seeking progressively greater challenges. This dynamic is particularly evident in skilled musicians, athletes, and programmers, who report flow as a central source of professional satisfaction.

다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

questions.passage: Proprioception — the body's internal sense of its own position and movement — is so fundamental to skilled physical action that most people are entirely unaware of its existence until it is disrupted. Unlike the five classical senses, proprioception does not involve specialized external receptors; instead, it relies on millions of sensory neurons embedded in muscles, tendons, and joints that continuously relay information about the body's configuration to the brain. Learning a new physical skill such as riding a bicycle or playing the piano is, at its core, a process of training the proprioceptive system. In the early stages, movements are slow and require conscious attention. With practice, the brain builds an accurate internal model of the body's dynamics, and movements that once demanded deliberate effort become automatic. This automatization frees conscious attention for higher-level aspects of performance. The proprioceptive system is also highly adaptable: it recalibrates rapidly in response to changes in the body, such as growth during adolescence, the wearing of prosthetic limbs, or even the donning of a new pair of shoes with a different heel height.

questions.passage: Economists have long struggled to quantify the value of biodiversity, largely because ecosystems provide services that lie entirely outside conventional market frameworks. A forest, for instance, simultaneously sequesters carbon, regulates local rainfall patterns, filters water, prevents soil erosion, houses pollinators essential to agriculture, and provides a genetic library of organisms that may yield future medicines. None of these functions carries a market price, yet their combined value to human well-being is immense. When natural habitats are converted to agricultural land or urban development, these services are lost — often permanently — but their loss rarely appears in economic calculations because they were never assigned a monetary value in the first place. Ecologists and environmental economists have developed a variety of methods to estimate these so-called "ecosystem services," including willingness-to-pay surveys, replacement cost analysis, and the hedonic pricing of properties adjacent to green spaces. Despite the imperfection of these methods, the consensus finding is unambiguous: the economic benefits provided by intact ecosystems far exceed the short-term financial gains from their destruction.

questions.passage: Researchers investigating how different cultures experience time have found striking variations in temporal orientation and time management practices. In many Western industrialized societies, time is conceptualized as a linear, finite resource — something that can be spent, wasted, saved, or lost. This metaphor of time as money shapes everyday behaviors from scheduling appointments in fifteen-minute increments to viewing punctuality as a moral virtue. In contrast, many cultures in Latin America, the Middle East, and sub-Saharan Africa operate on what anthropologists call "polychronic" time: multiple activities and social obligations are attended to simultaneously, schedules are treated as flexible rather than binding, and relationships are prioritized over adherence to clocks. Neither approach is inherently superior; each reflects the values of the culture that produced it. Problems arise primarily when individuals operating on different temporal frameworks must collaborate, as mismatches in time perception can generate mutual frustration, misattributions of rudeness, and breakdowns in communication.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

explanations.correct: (A) perception — 지문 전체가 시간을 어떻게 '인식(perceive)'하고 개념화하는지를 다루며, 단선적 자원 개념과 다중 활동 동시 처리 방식의 차이가 핵심이다. wrong: (A) measurement가 오답 — 지문은 단순한 시간 '측정'이 아니라 시간을 개념화하고 경험하는 문화적 방식 전반을 다룬다.
(B) innovation이 오답 — 지문은 서로 다른 시간 체계를 가진 사람들의 협업 시 문제를 다루며 혁신은 언급되지 않는다.

(A) scarcity가 오답 — 시간을 희소한 자원으로 보는 것은 서구 산업화 사회의 관점일 뿐, 지문이 다루는 모든 문화에 해당하지 않는다.

questions.passage: The acoustic properties of a space profoundly influence the behavior and emotional states of its occupants, often in ways they do not consciously notice. Research in environmental psychology has shown that high levels of ambient noise in open-plan offices reduce not only workers' ability to concentrate but also their willingness to communicate with colleagues, as the effort required for intelligible speech increases. Cafeterias designed with highly reverberant surfaces that amplify noise create a sense of urgency that causes diners to eat faster and leave sooner. By contrast, spaces with carefully managed acoustics — moderate reverberation times, sound-absorbing surfaces, and strategic barriers to noise — foster more deliberate behavior and promote longer, more satisfying social interactions. Hospitals that have incorporated acoustic design into patient ward planning have reported reductions in patient stress, faster recovery times, and lower staff turnover. These findings suggest that architects who attend to the sonic environment of a building are, in effect, designing not only the physical experience of space but also the quality of the human interactions that occur within it.

questions.passage: The story a society tells about its past is never a neutral record of events. Collective memory — the shared version of history that a community accepts as its own — is actively constructed and maintained through monuments, textbooks, commemorations, and national holidays. Historians have observed that collective memories tend to emphasize episodes of heroism, sacrifice, and victimhood while suppressing or minimizing events that are ambiguous, shameful, or morally complicated. The process of selection and omission is not merely innocent simplification; it serves the political function of creating a sense of common identity and shared destiny among citizens who have never met. This is why revisions to school history curricula are so politically charged: changing the story a society tells about itself threatens the emotional foundations of national cohesion. Conversely, societies that have undergone processes of historical reckoning — acknowledging past atrocities rather than erasing them — have generally produced more complex, but arguably more durable, forms of collective identity that can accommodate internal diversity rather than demanding uniformity.

questions.passage:
Epigenetics has overturned one of the central assumptions of classical genetics: that an organism's phenotype is determined solely by its genotype. Epigenetic mechanisms — including DNA methylation, histone modification, and non-coding RNA regulation — do not alter the DNA sequence itself but do control whether individual genes are expressed or silenced. These regulatory layers are sensitive to environmental inputs: diet, stress, exposure to toxins, and even social experience can leave lasting marks on the epigenome that alter gene expression patterns. What makes epigenetics particularly significant is the evidence that some epigenetic modifications are heritable — that is, they can be passed from parent to offspring across multiple generations without any change in the underlying DNA sequence. Studies in mice have shown that specific fears and metabolic predispositions induced by environmental stimuli in one generation can manifest in the grandchildren of the original animals, even when those grandchildren were raised in completely different conditions. These findings challenge the strict separation between inherited genetic endowment and acquired environmental influence that has defined biology since the Modern Synthesis of the mid-twentieth century.
* phenotype: 표현형 ** genotype: 유전형 *** histone: 히스톤(DNA를 감고 있는 단백질)

Epigenetics demonstrates that environmental factors can (A) gene expression without altering the DNA sequence, and that such modifications may be (B) across generations, blurring the boundary between inherited and acquired traits.

explanations.correct: (A) modify — 지문의 후성유전 메커니즘 설명인 "control whether individual genes are expressed or silenced"가 환경 요인이 유전자 발현을 'DNA 서열 변경 없이 조절(modify)'함을 직접 뒷받침한다. (B) transmitted — 지문의 "some epigenetic modifications are heritable — they can be passed from parent to offspring across multiple generations"와 쥐 실험에서 세대 간 전달이 확인되었다는 내용이 변형이 세대 간으로 '전달(transmitted)'됨을 명시한다.
wrong: (A) stabilize가 오답 — 후성유전은 유전자 발현을 안정화하는 것이 아니라 환경에 따라 동적으로 변화시킨다. (B) lost도 오답 — 지문은 후성유전적 변형이 사라지는 것이 아니라 세대를 넘어 유전됨을 강조한다.
(A) eliminate가 오답 — 후성유전 메커니즘은 유전자를 '제거'하는 것이 아니라 발현 여부를 조절한다.
(B) randomized가 오답 — 지문의 쥐 실험 결과는 후성유전적 변형이 무작위가 아니라 일관된 패턴으로 세대 간에 전달됨을 보여준다.
(A) determine이 오답 — 후성유전은 유전자 발현을 완전히 '결정'하는 것이 아니라 조절·변조한다. (B) suppressed도 오답 — 지문은 변형이 억제되는 것이 아니라 세대 간으로 유전됨을 서술한다.

questions.passage: Quantum mechanics describes subatomic particles in terms of wave functions — mathematical objects that encode the probabilities of finding a particle in various states when measured. Prior to measurement, a particle does not occupy a definite state; instead, it exists in a superposition of multiple possible states simultaneously. The act of measurement itself forces the wave function to "collapse" to one definite outcome. This feature of quantum theory, which has been experimentally confirmed with extraordinary precision, raises a philosophically troubling question: what constitutes a measurement? Some interpretations hold that measurement requires a conscious observer, a view that most physicists find unacceptable. The Copenhagen interpretation, the most widely taught, sidesteps the issue by treating the boundary between quantum system and measuring apparatus as a pragmatic rather than a fundamental divide. The Many-Worlds interpretation, developed by Hugh Everett III, resolves the collapse problem by proposing that every possible measurement outcome is realized in a separate branch of a constantly proliferating universe. Each interpretation preserves the predictive accuracy of quantum mechanics while offering radically different accounts of what measurement ultimately means for the nature of physical reality.
questions.question:
다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

While quantum mechanics' predictive power is (A) established, the meaning of measurement — which collapses a particle from superposition to a definite state — remains (B) debated across competing interpretations.

explanations.correct: (A) firmly — 지문의 "experimentally confirmed with extraordinary precision"이 양자역학의 예측력이 '확고히(firmly)' 확립되어 있음을 직접 뒷받침한다. (B) philosophically — 지문의 "raises a philosophically troubling question"과 측정의 의미가 '물리적 실재의 본질'에 대한 서로 다른 해석들 사이에서 여전히 논쟁 중이라는 내용이 논쟁이 '철학적으로(philosophically)' 이루어짐을 명시한다.
wrong: (A) weakly가 오답 — 지문은 양자역학의 예측력이 'extraordinary precision(탁월한 정밀도)'으로 실험적으로 확인되었다고 명시한다.

questions.passage:
The hypothesis of linguistic relativity, associated most closely with Benjamin Lee Whorf, proposes that the language one speaks does not merely reflect thought but actively shapes it — that the categories and distinctions encoded in a language influence the cognitive processes of its speakers. In its strong form, the hypothesis claims that certain thoughts are impossible without the linguistic structures to express them. Most linguists today reject the strong version, citing the ability of speakers to acquire entirely new conceptual frameworks when learning a second language. However, a weaker form of the hypothesis has received substantial empirical support. Speakers of languages that use absolute spatial directions (north, south, east, west) rather than relative ones (left, right, in front of, behind) show reliably superior spatial orientation skills, consistently pointing toward distant landmarks even in unfamiliar environments. Speakers of languages with a single term covering both blue and green perceive the boundary between the two colors differently from speakers of languages that mark the distinction lexically. These findings suggest that while language does not imprison thought, it does calibrate the default pathways through which habitual thinking tends to travel.
* lexically: 어휘적으로

(B) influence — 지문이 지지하는 약한 버전은 언어가 사고를 가두지는 않지만 "calibrate the default pathways through which habitual thinking tends to travel"한다는 것으로, 인지적 성향에 '영향(influence)'을 미침을 보여준다.

(A) simplifies가 오답 — 지문에서 부정되는 강한 주장은 언어의 '단순화' 역할이 아닌 사고의 '결정' 역할이다.
(B) replace가 오답 — 약한 버전의 가설은 언어가 사고를 '대체'하는 것이 아니라 기본 경로를 '조율'하는 데 그친다.
(A) reflects가 오답 — '언어가 사고를 반영한다'는 것은 워프 가설이 도전하는 관점이지, 가설 자체의 주장이 아니다.

questions.passage:
Regulatory capture is the process by which a regulatory agency, created to act in the public interest, becomes dominated by the very industry it was established to oversee and comes to act in that industry's interests rather than the public's. The phenomenon was first formally analyzed by economist George Stigler in the 1970s, who argued that regulation is typically sought by and designed for the benefit of industry rather than consumers. Capture occurs through several mechanisms. Regulated industries possess far superior information about their own operations than regulators, creating an asymmetry that agencies often rely on industry insiders to bridge — insiders who bring with them the priorities and assumptions of the industry. The "revolving door" between industry and regulatory agencies means that regulators frequently move into lucrative industry positions after leaving public service, creating incentives to maintain cooperative rather than adversarial relationships with those they supervise. Concentrated industry interests — focused on maximizing profit from a specific regulatory outcome — are typically better organized and better funded than diffuse public interests in safety and competition, giving them disproportionate influence over rule-making and enforcement.
* lucrative: 돈이 되는

questions.passage:
CRISPR-Cas9 technology has given scientists the ability to edit the genomes of living organisms with a precision and efficiency that was unimaginable a generation ago. By using a guide RNA to direct the Cas9 enzyme to a specific genomic location, researchers can cut, delete, or replace segments of DNA in virtually any cell type. The therapeutic potential of the technology is immense: early clinical trials have demonstrated durable remissions in patients with previously untreatable blood disorders, and researchers are actively exploring CRISPR-based treatments for cancers, genetic blindness, and infectious disease. However, the same capabilities that make CRISPR therapeutically promising also make it ethically perilous. When edits are made in somatic cells — those that constitute the body's tissues but are not passed on to descendants — the ethical concerns, while real, are broadly analogous to those raised by conventional drug treatments. Edits to germline cells — eggs, sperm, or early-stage embryos — are fundamentally different because they are heritable: every cell in any resulting individual will carry the modification, and it may be passed on indefinitely through subsequent generations. Critics argue that germline editing in humans crosses a qualitative boundary, creating not only unforeseen risks but also a potential avenue toward genetic enhancement that raises profound questions of equity and consent.
* somatic: 체세포의 ** germline: 생식세포계열의
